Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 1.  **Analyze the Request:**    *   **Input:** A pseudo-BPMN (Business Process Model and Notation) representation of a multinational smartphone supply chain. The process covers R&D, global sourcing, assembly in Malaysia, quality control, multi-modal shipping, customs, regional distribution, and inventory monitoring.    *   **Task:** Generate 20 open-ended, thought-provoking questions based on this process.    *   **Focus Areas:** Rationale behind flows, improvements, risk management, decision-making, stakeholder communication, performance measurement.    *   **Constraint:** No SQL queries.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 2.  **Deconstruct the Process Flow (Step-by-Step Analysis):**    *   *Start -> Market Research -> R&D/Design:* How does market research feed design? Is it agile?    *   *Parallel Sourcing (Taiwan, Japan, SK, Germany/USA):* Why these specific locations? What about geopolitical risks? Lead time variations? Currency fluctuations?    *   *Join -> Ship to Malaysia:* Why Malaysia? Consolidation logistics? Just-in-time feasibility?    *   *Assembly (24/7):* Labor laws? Fatigue management? Capacity constraints?    *   *Exclusive Gateway (Quality):* Scrap vs. Rework costs? Root cause analysis? Automation in QC?    *   *Pack & Label:* Customization here or earlier? Sustainability of packaging?    *   *Parallel Shipping (Air vs. Sea):* Cost/Benefit analysis? Carbon footprint? Speed-to-market priority?    *   *Customs:* Tariffs? Trade wars? Compliance automation?    *   *Distribution (Warehouse, Retail, E-com, VIP):* Channel conflict? Omnichannel strategy? Last-mile logistics?    *   *Inventory/Sales Forecast -> Adjustment Loop:* Bullwhip effect? AI in forecasting? Supplier flexibility?

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: 3.  **Brainstorming Questions by Category:**    *   *Category 1: Strategic Rationale & Design*        *   Q: Why Malaysia for assembly?        *   Q: Why specific component origins? (Concentration risk).        *   Q: Air vs. Sea split criteria?    *   *Category 2: Risk Management & Resilience*        *   Q: Geopolitical tensions (e.g., Taiwan strait) affecting the parallel gateway.        *   Q: Single point of failure at the Malaysia plant.        *   Q: Customs delays impact on the VIP/Direct shipment stream.    *   *Category 3: Operational Efficiency & Improvement*        *   Q: The "Rework" loop—how to minimize it? (Lean/Six Sigma).        *   Q: 24/7 Assembly sustainability.        *   Q: Synchronization of parallel sourcing (waiting for the slowest component).    *   *Category 4: Decision Making & Logic*        *   Q: Criteria for the Air vs. Sea decision (is it dynamic?).        *   Q: Thresholds for "Adjust Orders" vs "Next Cycle".    *   *Category 5: Stakeholder & Communication*        *   Q: Visibility for end-customers (especially VIPs).        *   Q: Coordination between Regional Distribution Centers.    *   *Category 6: Performance & Metrics*        *   Q: KPIs for the parallel distribution paths.        *   Q: Measuring the success of the "Market Research" phase against final sales.

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: 4.  **Drafting and Refining 20 Questions (Iterative Polishing):**    *   *Draft 1 (Sourcing):* Why buy chips from Taiwan? -> *Refinement:* Focus on the risk of geographic concentration in the parallel sourcing gateways.    *   *Draft 2 (Assembly):* Is 24/7 good? -> *Refinement:* Analyze the trade-off between throughput and defect rates in a continuous 24/7 operation.    *   *Draft 3 (Logistics):* Air freight is expensive. -> *Refinement:* Determine the algorithmic logic used to bifurcate shipments between high-cost air freight and slower sea freight; is it purely demand-based or margin-based?    *   *Draft 4 (The Loop):* The rework loop is bad. -> *Refinement:* Investigate the cost-benefit threshold where "Scrap" becomes more viable than "Rework" within the quality control gateway.    *   *Draft 5 (End of Cycle):* How do you forecast? -> *Refinement:* Evaluate the latency in the feedback loop between "Monitor Inventory" and "Adjust Orders," specifically regarding the Bullwhip Effect.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: 5.  **Final Selection & Organization (Ensuring Open-Endedness):**    *   Make sure they start with "How," "Why," "To what extent," "What if."    *   Ensure coverage of all requested domains (Rationale, Risk, etc.).    *(Self-Correction during drafting)*: I need to make sure I don't just ask "what happens" but "why is it designed this way" or "how can it be better."

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 6.  **Final Output Generation (mapping to the list below):**    1. Sourcing concentration risk.    2. Just-in-Time vs. Buffer stock (Malaysia).    3. Synchronization of parallel inputs.    4. Labor ethics/Efficiency in 24/7 ops.    5. QC loop optimization.    6. Carbon footprint of logistics split.    7. Dynamic routing (Air/Sea).    8. Customs as a bottleneck.    9. Channel conflict (Retail vs E-com).    10. VIP supply assurance.    11. Reverse logistics (implied by quality/rework).    12. Data integration across regions.    13. Currency hedging in global sourcing.    14. Intellectual Property protection during assembly.    15. Packaging standardization vs localization.    16. Supplier relationship depth.    17. Demand sensing accuracy.    18. Crisis simulation (e.g., Suez canal blockage).    19. Circular economy (end of life - though not in chart, relevant to 'next cycle').    20. Digital Twin feasibility for this model.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: 7.  **Review against Constraints:** No SQL? Checked. 20 questions? Checked. Thought-provoking? Yes.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: 8.  **Final Polish:** Present clearly with headings for readability. (Proceeding to generate output).
